item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it particularly because it gave it a bit of gravity… before that it was a relatively harmless SCP, whose consequences were fairly easy to avoid and manage. Sure, people felt doom, but going as far as to sever their finger? It made me go 'whoa, ok, maybe not so harmless'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Following the death of D-████████ after a candle was allowed to burn away completely in SCP-1064, all testing is to be put on hold until the cause of the subject’s death can be confirmed.

I have a theory:
Maybe this doesn't shorten people's lifespan by making them age faster, or makes people perceive it as such; maybe it shortens their lifespan (or makes them perceive that it's happening) by making the day that they die sooner.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
watching D-██████ remove her own finger by [DATA EXPUNGED] with the edge of a table was a fairly conclusive test result.

This is fairly stupid, why would you expunge something so irrelevant?

Other than that, it is well written and insteresting, upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image in this article was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. It may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact anqxyr or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The mental image at the end is both hilarious and deeply disturbing. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same with me; I was also wondering about a test with self-lighting candle. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The way I saw it: One makes people think their life is passing faster, the other that it is passing slower.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ambiguity for the win.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Minor quible, the description says pink, but the ring in the picture is a dark red/purpley color.

Otherwise, very nice first try.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1. The addendum at the end did it for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The two effects are so ridiculously not related, I almost didn't want to upvote, but that final note sold it. I like that it just seems to make people think that something is happening (perhaps strongly enough to convince them they're dead, like that D-class?). It's just so bizarre.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I gotta admit, when I was reading this, some part of my brain went "Huh, I wonder what happens when you put one of those self-lighting candles in it?"

And then, you answered that question: pants-shitting (or in this case, finger-smashing-off) insanity. Bravo, ESPECIALLY for a first-run!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel similarly; it was the last bit that secured my upvote as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm honoured :) I considered it, but wasn't sure it'd be up to scratch and thought that it'd be a bit… I don't know, as if I'd barged in with my first attempt and started appropriating long-running things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll have a think and see if I can add some more to it Soullesshuman. I'm glad you found it interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good point Khym Chanur, I'll change that now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the advice Vezaz :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That was me trying not to get carried away with containent procedures; I'll have a think and see if I can add something else to them.

Edit: I've tried to extend the containment procedures a bit now, but I'm not sure what else I could add if anything; suggestions are, of course, very welcome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it's your first SCP, eh? well, then join the very small club of people who have gotten an upvote from me on their first outing. My only suggestion: Have you considered making this a 'The Factory' item?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's simple, yet interesting. I do wish it explained a bit more; I feel like there's a tiny thing that I'm not getting and that article is just barely hinting at but I'm missing it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You might want to change the image caption to something like "SCP-1064 with attached candle", so even if you only read the caption it's clear that the candle isn't a part of the SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This stopped me from upvoting, to be honest. It doesn't even say where it's kept!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I will Raven :) I was nervous enough that I made a mess of the tags and noting why I'd edited… I'll make sure I don't next time round.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I second this. It was this last part that secured my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In the future, please check out the Attribute Tag Guide for the list of acceptable tags. It helps us not have to go back through it again. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would say keep 1064-A, perhaps simply saying "is not recommended" rather than "is not permitted." It puts a good sting on the end of a seemingly innocent object.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one's a hoot. The addendums work well too. I would advise not using strict percentages for the effects, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair point, I'll remove that section.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Minor issue:

Addendum 1064-A: Dr ███████ - Inserting novelty relighting candles into SCP-1064 is not to be permitted. As far as I am concerned, watching D-██████ remove her own finger by [DATA EXPUNGED] with the edge of a table was a fairly conclusive test result.

This note has little merit. While it may be true that further experimentation in this regard may be unnescessary (although it isn't, because one test can never provide a conclusive result), I can think of no reason that it would be outright disallowed, expecially since it presents no direct danger to others.

Also, its generally good form to put such text in italics, with the signature at the end - although this is a convention, not a rule.

Anyway, apart from that, I like this. It's one of those quaint, simple little oddities which I so adore, and the writing is compotent (which is more than can be said for some other first articles). Keep at it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Containment procedures are a little short, but I like it. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My first attempt; thanks in advance for your constructive criticism guys.
